frameworks.name: perceptual reality monitoring
frameworks.one_line: functionalist account distinguishing conscious from non-conscious perceptual representations

frameworks.name: higher-order theory of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: empirical variant treated as the theoretical basis of reality monitoring

frameworks.name: alternative theories of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: competitors evaluated against the proposed account on plausibility

core_question: What mechanism accounts for consciousness in humans, and does it extend to animals or robots?
key_assumptions: consciousness has identifiable mechanisms in the human case
inductive generalization to other systems requires a theory of consciousness
consciousness is multiply realizable and functionalist in character
interpretations: some animals may lack consciousness while suitably organized robots or programs could possess it
perceptual reality monitoring should be evaluated against rival theories by plausibility
out_of_scope_note: book chapter, theoretical / philosophical, no empirical findings
